name: Pumpernickel Bagels
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Skim milk, scalded and cooled to room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small skillet, bring 3 tablespoons sugar to a boil over moderate heat until it melts into a rich, dark brown liquid.
2. Carefully add the boiling water and stir to dissolve the caramel. Pour into a dish to cool.
3. Combine warm water, yeast, and remaining teaspoon sugar in a large mixing bowl. Let stand foamy for about 10 minutes.
4. In the same bowl, mix salt, melted butter, cooled milk, and liquid caramel with the yeast mixture.
5. Gradually add the Pumpernickel and rye flours, then 2 cups of all-purpose flour. Sprinkle 1 cup of the remaining all-purpose flour on a work surface and turn the dough out onto it.
6. Knead in additional flour as needed to form a fairly stiff dough. Continue kneading until the dough is no longer sticky.
7. Form the dough into a ball, place in a greased bowl, turning once or twice. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place until doubled.
8. Punch down the dough, divide it in half, and roll each half into 2 10-inch cylinders. Cover one towel and let rest for 10 minutes.
9. Roll each cylinder into a snake about 8 inches long, tapering at the ends. Wet both ends, overlap about an inch, and gently pinch the ends together.
10. Place the bagels on two baking sheets covered with parchment paper, cover with a towel, and let proof until nearly doubled.
11. Brush tops with egg wash and place in a 425°F oven on the center racks. Bake for 10 minutes, then switch pans and bake an additional 5 to 10 minutes until well browned.
12. Cool on wire racks. Store in plastic bags up to 2 days. Longer – freeze.
